audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua first Joshua farewell address, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Joshua's old age, rest from enemies, remaining nations, Torah adherence, non-assimilation warning, love of YHWH, intermarriage prohibition, good-word fulfillment, and covenant-loss threat.

verses.restored: It was many days after YHWH had given rest to Israel from all their enemies around, and Joshua was old, advanced in days,
that Joshua called to all Israel, to its elders, heads, judges, and officers, and said to them, I am old, advanced in days.
You have seen all YHWH your Divine did to all these nations before you, for YHWH your Divine is the one who fought for you.
See, I have caused to fall to you these remaining nations as inheritance for your tribes, from Jordan, and all nations I cut off, as far as Great Sea toward going down of sun.
YHWH your Divine, he will thrust them out from before you and dispossess them from before you, and you shall possess their land, as YHWH your Divine spoke to you.
Be very strong to keep and do all written in scroll of Torah of Moses, not turning from it right or left,
so you do not enter among these nations, these remaining with you. Name of their gods you shall not mention, and by them you shall not swear, serve them, or bow to them.
But to YHWH your Divine you shall cling, as you have done to this day.
YHWH dispossessed from before you nations great and mighty; as for you, no man has stood before you to this day.
One man from you pursues a thousand, because YHWH your Divine is the one fighting for you, as he spoke to you.
Guard yourselves greatly for your souls/lives, to love YHWH your Divine.
For if you turn back and cling to remnant of these nations, these remaining with you, and make marriages with them, and go in to them and they to you,

know surely that YHWH your Divine will not continue to dispossess these nations from before you; they shall become for you snare, trap, whip in your sides, and thorns in your eyes, until you perish from upon this good ground YHWH your Divine gave you.

Look, I am going today in way of all earth. You know with all your heart and all your soul/life that not one word has fallen from all good words YHWH your Divine spoke concerning you; all came for you; not one word fell from it.

It shall be, as every good word came upon you that YHWH your Divine spoke to you, so YHWH will bring upon you every evil word until he destroys you from upon this good ground YHWH your Divine gave you,

when you cross covenant of YHWH your Divine that he commanded you and go and serve other gods and bow to them; anger of YHWH will burn against you, and you shall perish quickly from upon good land he gave you.

translation_notes: Joshua again speaks as an aging leader whose authority is used to warn and transfer responsibility, not to pretend immortality.
The address openly acknowledges remaining nations living with Israel, complicating earlier total-conquest summaries.
The warning against naming, serving, and swearing by other gods belongs to Israel's covenant boundary and must not authorize persecution of other religions or suppression of speech today.
The intermarriage warning is ancient religious-boundary rhetoric, not a modern ethnic-purity rule or permission for racism, xenophobia, or control of intimate relationships.
Images of snare, trap, whip, and thorns are covenant-danger metaphors and must not be used to dehumanize neighboring peoples or mixed families.
Fulfillment language in verse 14 is immediately paired with warning in verses 15-16; it is theological rhetoric, not simple historical accounting.
Love of YHWH is framed as guarded covenant allegiance, but love cannot be coerced through domination or fear.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 23 to promote ethnic purity, anti-intermarriage ideology, religious persecution, racism, or contempt for neighboring peoples. Center aging leadership that releases responsibility, disciplined love, covenant memory, and honest recognition that communities remain mixed and ethically accountable.
